content:
Key Findings

  • Fylde Borough Council's Strategic Development department spent £47.4M between 2015/16 and 2025/26
  • This represents 30.1% of the council's total spending of £157.4M
  • Strategic Development is the highest-spending department, ahead of Community Services (£34.4M) and Governance and Partnerships (£33.2M)
  • The Chief Executive's office spent £1.4M, just 0.9% of the total
  • Corporate Policy and Performance spent the least, with £5,921 over the 11-year period

Strategic Development's Spending Dominance

Fylde Borough Council's Strategic Development department stands out as the single largest area of expenditure, accounting for £47.4M over the 11-year period from 2015/16 to 2025/26. This represents a substantial 30.1% of the council's total spending of £157.4M, highlighting its central role in the local authority's financial priorities.

The department's spending dwarfs that of other areas, with the next highest, Community Services, receiving £34.4M (21.9% of total spending). Governance and Partnerships follows closely with £33.2M (21.1%). This significant investment in Strategic Development suggests a strong focus on long-term planning and growth initiatives within the council's budgetary strategy.

Departmental Spending Breakdown

The distribution of spending across Fylde Borough Council's departments reveals clear priorities. While Strategic Development leads with £47.4M, other areas also receive notable allocations. Operational Services, for example, received £26.2M (16.7%), indicating significant investment in day-to-day operational needs.

At the other end of the spectrum, departments like Community and Cultural Services, Democratic Services and Member Support, and Streetscene Services received minimal funding, with £104K, £14K, and £2,500 respectively. This stark contrast underscores the council's focus on strategic initiatives over smaller-scale services.

Comparing Departmental Expenditure

The following table provides a clear comparison of the spending across Fylde Borough Council's departments, highlighting the dominance of Strategic Development and the relatively lower investments in other areas.

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 STRATEGIC DEVELOPMENT £47.4M 30.1%
2 COMMUNITY SERVICES £34.4M 21.9%
3 GOVERNANCE AND PARTNERSHIPS £33.2M 21.1%
4 OPERATIONAL SERVICES £26.2M 16.7%
5 STRATEGIC PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT £14.6M 9.3%
6 CHIEF EXECUTIVE £1.4M 0.9%
7 COMMUNITY AND CULTURAL SERVICES £104K 0.1%
8 DMOCRTIC SVCS AND MEMBER SUPPT £14K 0.0%
9 CORPORATE POLICY AND PERFORMANCE £5,921 0.0%
10 STREETSCENE SERVICES £2,500 0.0%

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Fylde Borough Council about the specific projects funded by the Strategic Development department
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about the Strategic Development department's plans and priorities
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers are benefiting from the Strategic Development budget
  • Write to your local councillor to ask about the impact of the Strategic Development department's spending on local services
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends across all departments
This article is based on Fylde Borough Council's published spending data covering 2015/16 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
